title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing · Object-Lifetime Lens: Tool Limit Matrix
summary: A security parser needs fast local feedback, nightly deep instrumentation, adversarial generation, and reproducible findings. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem tool limit matrix increment in layered memory-defect test matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.
Build a deterministic libFuzzer target with bounded input, sanitizer instrumentation, corpus, dictionary, timeout, and minimized regressions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Tool Limit Matrix · Theory Tool Limit Matrix
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 6642. Produce bounded C evidence to classify a defect against complementary native tools; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Tool Limit Matrix contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Tool Limit Matrix evidence · Theory Tool Limit Matrix
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-tool-limit-matrix, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-tool-limit-matrix-1jmzpo */" for changed case 3824. It must classify a defect against complementary native tools, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails.
